description: Recurring due-work loading no longer joins `client_contract_lines` for persisted client-cadence rows.
Recurring materialization-gap detection no longer queries active client-cadence obligations from `client_contract_lines`.
`AutomaticInvoices` loads successfully in fully migrated environments where `client_contract_lines` does not exist.
Recurring preview selector normalization resolves client-cadence service periods without joining `client_contract_lines`.
Recurring generate selector normalization resolves client-cadence service periods without joining `client_contract_lines`.
Recurring service-period inspection resolves client-cadence obligation metadata from the surviving client-owned structure.
Client-cadence recurring regeneration loads obligations from the surviving structure instead of `client_contract_lines`.
Invoice-to-service-period linkage no longer derives live recurring obligation candidates from `client_contract_lines`.
Bucket recurring period resolution no longer depends on `client_contract_lines`.
Contract wizard no longer inserts rows into dropped `client_contract_lines` or `client_contract_services` tables.
Contract line mutation/runtime service paths stop requiring the dropped `client_contract_lines` structure for live behavior.
Credit and other dependent billing flows stop reading dropped client-contract line tables in live paths.
Billing/report read models stop using `client_contract_lines` as a live fact source.
One canonical post-drop recurring obligation identity is defined and used consistently for client cadence.
Legacy `client_contract_line` identity, if retained at all, is limited to documented passive compatibility metadata rather than live table joins.
Static tests and integration tests are updated so they no longer assert `client_contract_lines` as expected live runtime behavior.
Stale plan/runbook/docs references to `client_contract_lines` as a live runtime dependency are corrected or marked historical.
No live runtime path crashes with `relation \"client_contract_lines\" does not exist` in a fully migrated environment.
Server-side contract-line service paths stop using `client_contract_lines` for live behaviors such as unassign/deactivate, usage analytics, in-use checks, overlap validation, and overview counts.
Cleanup-only test fixtures, teardowns, tracked backup artifacts, and stale operational runbooks stop pointing active development flows at dropped client-contract line tables.
